data.title: Syntheia announces shares-for-debt transaction
data.description:
• Syntheia (SYAIF) on Thursday said it will pay $0.1M of debt to a creditor by issuing 0.83M common shares.
  • The debt settlement is subject to corporate and regulatory approvals, and the shares issued will be subject to a four-month hold period.

data.title: Roche upgraded at Goldman Sachs on reduced catalyst risks
data.description:
• Goldman Sachs upgraded Roche (RHHBY) to neutral from sell saying that the Swiss pharma is facing reduced catalyst risks this year as well as improved growth potential.
  • The price target was raised to CHF365 from CHF260 (~6% upside based on Thursday's close on the SIX Swiss Exchange.) 
  • Analysts at the investment bank said that Roche ended 2025 on a high note given a positive readout on a fenebrutinib trial in relapsing multiple sclerosis, positive results on the asset in primary progressive MS, and an early positive readout from a trial for giredestrant in the adjuvant setting. "These successes provide positive read-across for key 2026 trials, specifically giredestrant in the 1L setting and the second fenebrutinib trial in RMS."
  • The team added that it believes "the scientific risks for the obesity Phase 2 data release for CT-388, CT-996, and petrelintide are relatively low."

data.title: QXO announces $750M common stock offering
data.description:
• QXO (NYSE:QXO) stated on Thursday that it intends to offer $750M of common stock, with the underwriter having an option to purchase up to an additional $112.5M of shares of common stock.
  • Proceeds from the offering will be used to fund corporate purposes and potential acquisitions.
  • BofA Securities is acting as the sole underwriter for the offering.
  • Shares -1.96%.

data.title: Coinbase's Armstrong lobbies lawmakers to preserve stablecoin rewards - report
data.description:
Coinbase Global (COIN) CEO Brian Armstrong spent Thursday on Capitol Hill urging lawmakers to protect his crypto exchange's ability to offer reward on stablecoins, as part of his opposition to the Senate Banking Committee's crypto market structure draft bill.

"Maybe the biggest" concern with the bill is that it could undermine competition between crypto firms and traditional banks by limiting rewards for stablecoins, Armstrong told reporters at the Capitol, Bloomberg reported.

On Wednesday, Armstrong said his company cannot back the revised legislation that Senate Banking Committee Chairman Tim Scott released earlier in the week. Scott subsequently said the committee delayed action on the bill.

Armstrong on Thursday warned that advancing the bill would be premature, noting that senators were poised to vote on amendments that would essentially ban stablecoin rewards, Bloomberg reported.

“We do think that Americans should be able to earn more money on their money, and the banks right now are worried about that — they’re worried they’re going to have to start paying higher rates to customers to compete,” he said.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: QXO expects Q4 net sales of $2.19B
data.description:
• QXO (QXO) announced on Thursday that the company expects Q4 net sales of approximately $2.19B versus a consensus of $2.20B and adjusted EBITDA of approximately $150M.
  • QXO +0.67% after hours to $25.19.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Granite leads joint venture on 22nd Street widening
data.description:
• Granite (NYSE: GVA) announced  on Thursday as the lead partner in the Granite-Traylor Joint Venture that it was selected by the City of Tucson for the 22nd Street Widening and Bridge Replacement Project in Arizona.
  • The company’s $157M Tucson project, funded by the city, regional authority, and $25M RAISE grant, will appear in the Q4 2025 capital plan.
  • 22nd Street will be rebuilt from Kino Parkway to Tucson Boulevard, with the 4-lane bridge replaced by a modern 6-lane structure.

data.title: WaFd Non-GAAP EPS of $0.75 misses by $0.01, revenue of $191.37M misses by $1.46M
data.description:
• WaFd press release (WAFD): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.75 misses by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $191.37M (+11.7% Y/Y) misses by $1.46M.

data.title: Ibderdrola asks court to block Trump's halt of Massachusetts offshore wind project
data.description:
Iberdrola (IBDRY) (IBDSF) and Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners asked a U.S. court Thursday to block the Trump administration's suspension of their Vineyard Wind offshore wind project off the coast of Massachusetts.

The joint venture is seeking a temporary restraining order and injunction to lift the Interior Department's December 22 pause of the project.

The JV said Vineyard Wind, which was expected to be finished by March 31, is 95% complete and already sending power to the grid, but is losing $2M daily because of the government order.

Vineyard Wind was one of five offshore wind projects halted by the Trump administration in December over purported national security concerns.

The court filing came the same day that a federal judge allowed the New York-based Empire Wind project to resume construction.

ETF: (FAN)

data.title: SunCoke Energy names Shantanu Agrawal as CFO
data.description:
• SunCoke Energy (SXC) said  on Thursday that Shantanu Agrawal, currently VP of Finance and Treasurer, will succeed Mark W. Marinko as CFO, effective March 13, 2026.
  • Agrawal brings over a decade of finance and accounting experience and deep expertise in the company's operations. He joined the company as an FP&A analyst in 2014 and has served as VP of Finance and Treasurer since 2021.

data.title: Frontier Comm ticks higher after Calif. PUC approves Verizon deal
data.description:
Frontier Communications (FYBR) edged higher by 0.3% after the California Public Utilities Commission voted to approve its sale to Verizon (VZ).

The Calif. PUC voted 5-0 to approve the transaction  on Thursday. 

The Federal Communications Commission approved Verizon's (NYSE:VZ) $9.6 billion purchase of Frontier (NASDAQ:FYBR) in May after the telecom firm agreed to end its diversity, equity and inclusion policies.
